comment.body: One point I disagree with Alma is that Trump is a savy operator in that environment. I don't think he is smart and seems to lack the necessary strategic planning for this position. And his mighty band of amateurs he has surrounded himself with are mostly not better. But he certainly is street smart. And there are certainly people around him that know what they are doing. And he knows the levers to pull if he thinks he has the better hand. And he plays the card, even with a high probability of loss. That's gambling, not savy politics. His business track record does not indicate strategic genius and if it wasn't for some shady Russian businessmen who bailed him out a couple of times in the 80s, 90s and 00s we would probably not talk about him anymore...

comment.body:
Not sure that's possible?  We really should've entered into a significant recession after Covid, but that didn't happen under the Biden administration when they brought in all those illegal immigrants and gave them free money, food and board, as well as propped up employment through government hirings.  This pushed up aggregate market demand and essentially kicked the can down the road by inflating the bubble.  

Sooner or later the bubble is gonna pop.  But the more you inflate it, the more disastrous it becomes.  Trump deflating the bubble now is gonna be better for Americans in the long run, though they may not see it that way.
